title: Sandbox Session Lifecycle

steps: Check if agent_id already in _acquired_agents → if yes, return (idempotent)
Acquire guard.lock → if guard.active_runs > 0 → raise RuntimeError (concurrent reuse forbidden)
Call _ensure_resume_key(agent) to assign stable resume key
Look up existing resources for agent_id in _resources_by_agent
[DECISION] If resources is None → call _create_resources(agent, capabilities, is_resumed_state)
[PATH A — Live Injected Session] If sandbox_config.session is not None:
→ await session.running() to check if already running
→ If processed_manifest changed: update session.state with new manifest
[PATH B — Resume from state] If session_state or run_state resume payload available:
→ _resume_state_payload_for_agent(client, agent, agent_id) → lookup sessions_by_agent by resume key or agent name
→ _process_resumed_state_manifest(agent, capabilities, session_state) → apply capability manifest transforms
[PATH C — Fresh session] No session or state:
→ _process_manifest(capabilities, manifest, run_as_user) → apply capability transforms + add user
→ Check sandbox_config.options (required if client.supports_default_options is False)
[DECISION] If _started AND await session.running() → return (already running)
[DECISION] If NOT owns_session AND await session.running() → set _started=True, return
Load existing_payload from run_state._sandbox (deep copy) if available
[DECISION] If sandbox_config is None → return existing_payload (pass-through)
[DECISION] If sandbox_config.session is not None (injected live session) → return None (no resume state)
[DECISION] If sandbox_config.client is None → return existing_payload
Return dict with: backend_id, current_agent_key, current_agent_name, session_state, sessions_by_agent
Open TRACE custom_span('sandbox.cleanup_sessions') if resources exist and tracing active
→ If client is not None AND session is SandboxSession: await client.delete(session)
→ Accumulate first error; continue remaining cleanup steps regardless
_release_agents(): decrement guard.active_runs for all acquired agents, clear bookkeeping dicts

key_conditions_and_decisions: Concurrent reuse guard: SandboxAgent cannot be used in >1 run simultaneously (RuntimeError)
owns_session flag: injected live sessions are NOT stopped/deleted on cleanup; created/resumed sessions ARE
Idempotent cleanup: _cleaned flag + _cleanup_lock prevents double-cleanup races
Resume key assignment priority: stable IR-derived key > prior run's current_agent_key > agent name deduplication > allocated unique key
Backend ID validation: RunState sandbox backend must match configured client (ValueError on mismatch)
Snapshot fallback: resolve_default_local_snapshot_spec() → falls back to NoopSnapshotSpec() on OSError
Live session manifest restrictions: cannot change root/environment/users/groups/mounts on a running injected session; new Dir entries allowed if no mounts
key_data_structures: _resume_keys_by_agent_id: dict[int, str] — stable resume key per agent
SandboxSessionState: Pydantic model with session_id(UUID), snapshot, manifest, exposed_ports, workspace_root_ready
serialize_resume_state payload: {backend_id, current_agent_key, current_agent_name, session_state, sessions_by_agent}
key_relationships: SandboxRuntimeSessionManager reads/writes RunState._sandbox for cross-run resume state
Capability.process_manifest() transforms Manifest during session creation/resume
tracing_integration: SandboxSession._annotate() wraps every session op with start/finish audit events + trace spans
important_notes: SandboxRuntimeSessionManager is Generic[TContext]. The class is the bridge between Runner run-config (SandboxRunConfig) and actual session lifecycle. Resume state is serialized as a dict and stored in RunState._sandbox, enabling cross-run session persistence. Concurrency is guarded per-agent via a threading.Lock-protected counter. The three _create_resources paths are mutually exclusive: injected session > resume state > fresh creation.
